focus:
Focus on vector database systems, approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search infrastructure, and embedding storage/retrieval engines — with an emphasis on system design, indexing algorithms, query processing, and engineering trade-offs, rather than the embedding models themselves.

Included Content (In Scope)

Vector database architectures and systems (e.g., Milvus, Weaviate, Qdrant, Pinecone, pgvector, Faiss, HNSW variants)
ANN index structures: HNSW, IVF, LSH, graph-based, tree-based, product quantization, and hybrid indexes
Vector compression and quantization techniques for storage efficiency (PQ, SQ, RabitQ, etc.)
Query processing and optimization for approximate and exact nearest neighbor search
Scalability, distributed, and cloud-native designs for vector search at scale
Filtered vector search: combining metadata filters with ANN search
Multi-vector and sparse-dense hybrid search (e.g., combining BM25 with dense embeddings)
Hardware-aware optimizations: SIMD, GPU, FPGA, memory hierarchy for vector search
Benchmarking, evaluation frameworks, and datasets specifically for vector search systems
Ingestion pipelines, indexing throughput, update/delete support in vector databases
Consistency, durability, and transactional properties of vector database engines
Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) infrastructure with a focus on the retrieval/vector store layer
Vectorization and embedding management as a database primitive
Open-source vector database systems, tools, and libraries

Excluded Content (Out of Scope)

Embedding model training, pre-training, or fine-tuning without a database/search system contribution
General representation learning or self-supervised learning papers
NLP, CV, or multi-modal papers that use vector search as an incidental tool
Recommender systems using embeddings without a vector search infrastructure angle
Knowledge graph embedding methods without a retrieval system contribution
General RAG papers focused on prompting or generation quality rather than the retrieval layer
General information retrieval (e.g., BM25-only, inverted index) without vector search
Application papers using off-the-shelf vector DBs without system-level contributions
